title: Antheropeas lanosum
taxonomy: Antheropeas lanosum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Antheropeas lanosum is classified as a type of legume. Legumes are a group of plants that produce seed pods and are often nitrogen-fixing, which makes them beneficial for the environment. Antheropeas lanosum is also sometimes referred to as a type of shrub.

intro: Antheropeas lanosum, commonly known as hairy desert marigold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations and supporting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Antheropeas lanosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Antheropeas lanosum is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
